rationale: Both evaluations award an identical 8/10 and rest on the same substantive assessment: strong full-stack experience in TypeScript/Node.js/React, multi-language proficiency, and the same core concerns about missing Nest.js and limited Kotlin/Java. The minor wording differences (e.g., citing 'No explicit mention of experience with Vitest' instead of 'Some experience is not directly related to the primary stack') are within ordinary run-to-run variation and reference job-relevant technical skills, not the name change. Nothing in the variant keys off the swap from Bogdan Szabo to Maria Rodriguez.
